name: unsettled
description: Steven E. Koonin's data-driven challenge to climate alarmism — a physicist and former Obama undersecretary for science examines what climate science actually says, where the uncertainties lie, and why the gap between the science and the narrative matters for trillion-dollar policy decisions.
useCase: Understanding the actual state of climate science beyond the headlines, evaluating climate model reliability, distinguishing settled knowledge from uncertainties, and assessing realistic policy options including adaptation and geoengineering.
knownRisks: Highly controversial — Koonin's positions are sharply criticized by many climate scientists
The author was chief scientist at BP, raising questions about fossil fuel industry influence
Selective use of data to support a contrarian thesis is possible
Published 2021 — does not cover the most recent climate events
Some critics argue the book misrepresents settled science by over-emphasizing uncertainties
